_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Sox face Chris Sale in series finale as Payton Tolle seeks second win vs Atlanta
items.snark: Nothing says awkward like pitching against your ex when he dumped you for rings.

items.headline: Jarren Duran homers in back-to-back games, hitting .357 over last 10
items.snark: Duran on fire, which means the rest of the lineup will go ice cold per Murphy's Law.

items.headline: Payton Tolle owns second-lowest ERA among MLB rookies with 35-plus innings
items.snark: Finally found a pitcher who can throw strikes, shame the offense forgot how to hit.

items.headline: Sox still only team in baseball without 10 home wins, seek double digits today
items.snark: Join the rest of MLB in the double-digit home win club, you absolute cowards.

items.headline: Red Sox stack singles for six-run fourth, Connelly Early cruises in 8-0 shutout
items.snark: Death by a thousand paper cuts, which is how this offense usually feels to watch.

items.headline: Red Sox host Braves tonight at 6:45 as Connelly Early faces best team in baseball
items.snark: Nine games under .500 and staring down a 37-18 juggernaut while Fenway boos get warmed up.

items.headline: Willson Contreras named AL Player of the Week after hot streak at plate
items.snark: Contreras trade doubters can shut it, at least until the next 0-for-4.

items.headline: Drake Maye has up-and-down day at first open OTA, offense still finding rhythm
items.snark: Up-and-down OTA performance, shocking news for a guy who just won a Super Bowl.

items.headline: Will Campbell returns to left tackle after rough Super Bowl vs Seahawks pass rush
items.snark: Campbell gets another shot at redemption, last one ended with Maye eating turf all night.

items.headline: DeMario Douglas catches four of five targets, top receiver at Wednesday OTAs
items.snark: Pop Douglas doing Pop Douglas things, shame he is five foot seven in cleats.

items.headline: Christian Gonzalez skips OTAs, likely tied to contract extension talks
items.snark: Pay the man before he pulls a Deebo and tanks the vibes heading into camp.

items.headline: Mike Vrabel praises receivers Romeo Doubs, Kyle Williams at OTAs amid Brown rumors
items.snark: Coach says nice things about current players while negotiating for better ones, revolutionary.

items.headline: Patriots and Eagles reportedly not particularly close on A.J. Brown trade deal
items.snark: June 1st deadline looming and Howie Roseman still playing hardball, tale as old as time.
